rationale.R1.1: Positioning against prior work centrally involves reframing the contribution claim and design-space location relative to existing systems.
rationale.R1.4: The delegation is load-bearing on assessing and articulating the delta between AgentFlow and the nearest prior work.
rationale.R1.7: Prior-work positioning requires attention to relevant literature coverage, attribution, and whether the cited lines of work are appropriate.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty where nearest prior systems or literature coverage cannot be fully verified from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The broad claims that the design point is previously unaddressed and that no prior system used this scheduling unit require overclaim control.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to prior-work positioning rather than executing edits, evaluating experiments, or redesigning the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Mispositioning prior work could unfairly diminish existing systems or mislead downstream readers about novelty.
rationale.RX.5: Claims about differentiation and absence of prior systems should be traceable to named prior-work categories or citations.
